mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive momentum in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates broad economic strength, leading to higher expected corporate earnings and positively influencing equity valuations over the medium term. This is an always-on signal reflecting the health of the real economy.
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS value is 151314.0, indicating a large workforce.
failure_condition: The signal's walk-forward rank information coefficient (IC) remains below 0.02 over any continuous 3-year period when evaluated on a monthly basis.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp acceleration in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) above a critical threshold forces the central bank to tighten monetary policy more aggressively than anticipated, increasing the discount rate for equities and causing negative forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Current CPIAUCSL is 288.663, a historically elevated level.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where the trigger condition is met over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of slowing industrial production (INDPRO) and rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals a stagflationary environment. This compresses corporate profit margins and increases economic uncertainty, leading to negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The dual-proxy signal fails to generate a statistically significant negative mean forward return (p > 0.05) over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: In a low interest rate regime, a sharp contraction in housing starts (HOUST) serves as a leading indicator of a broader slowdown in consumer demand and construction, negatively impacting forward-looking earnings expectations and equity prices.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS is 0.33, indicating a low-rate regime is active.
failure_condition: The negative relationship between HOUST contractions and S&P 500 returns disappears or reverses when conditioning on FEDFUNDS being below 2%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong upside surprises or accelerating trends in retail sales (RSAFS) signal robust consumer spending, which directly boosts revenue expectations for consumer-facing companies and drives positive short-term market sentiment.
evidence_refs: fact:Current RSAFS value is 667724.0, representing significant consumer activity.
failure_condition: The average 5-day S&P 500 return following the release of RSAFS data showing positive 3-month momentum is not statistically different from zero.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above a level indicating significant labor market slack, it increases the probability of future monetary policy accommodation, which lowers discount rates and supports higher equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE of 3.6 is low, so this signal is not currently active.
failure_condition: In historical periods where UNRATE was above 5.0%, the average 20-day forward S&P 500 return was negative or statistically indistinguishable from periods where UNRATE was below 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: An accommodative monetary policy stance, proxied by a low absolute level of the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS), reduces borrowing costs for corporations and encourages investor risk-taking, providing a tailwind for equity prices.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS value of 0.33 meets the trigger condition.
failure_condition: The correlation between the level of FEDFUNDS (when below 1.0%) and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is negative over a 10-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong growth in real GDP (GDPC1) reflects a fundamentally healthy and expanding economy. This translates into broad-based corporate revenue and earnings growth, justifying higher equity market valuations over the subsequent quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 value is 19735.895, representing the latest quarterly output.
failure_condition: The beta of 60-day forward S&P 500 returns to the quarter-over-quarter percentage change in GDPC1 is not statistically greater than zero (p > 0.05) over a full business cycle.
